name: mixed_ruby_bouten

meta.note: [provenance:hand-derived §6.1+§6.2+§7.5 + AOZORA-ANNOTATION 2026-06-17; amended for reference-impl #384/ADR-0023] The only 青梅 preceding the bracket is the ruby base, so the forward bouten target resolves to it: a non-adjacent target that is itself a ruby base. Per ADR-0023 (amending ADR 0003 §6.2 / ADR 0019), when the target is the UNIQUE such occurrence (no competing plain-text copy) the emphasis is applied to the ruby base in place as a render-only decoration: HTML renders 青梅 once — inside the ruby base — now wrapped in the bouten emphasis element (the #228 no-double-render invariant holds; the target is not re-rendered). The directive stays a forward reference and the decoration is render-only, never serialized, so serialize re-emits the bracket form unchanged with the literal left upstream (the bouten node still exists, the bracket round-trips). Because the styling IS applied, §7.5 raises NO forward-referent-not-stylable (a non-unique target — a ruby base that also appears as plain text, or two same-base rubies — stays declined and keeps the warning). Offsets from the §3 byte model. Serialize canonicalises right-side ruby to the BARE form (the explicit ｜ is dropped) per ADR 0003 §6.1 / ADR 0002: ｜ is emitted only when a bare 《reading》 would re-parse to a different base; here every base is an all-kanji run whose preceding char is non-base (line start / 　 / の), so the bare form re-parses identically.

source: ｜青梅《おうめ》には［＃「青梅」に傍点］という街道がある。

expected.html: 青梅(おうめ)にはという街道がある。
expected.serialize: 青梅《おうめ》には［＃「青梅」に傍点］という街道がある。
